commands.review_summary:
The PR renames private reported unsupported tool-schema fixture identifiers to synthetic `fuzzplugin` / `fuzz_move_angles` names across agent, Codex, gateway-protocol, and doctor tests.

PR surface: Tests -2. Total -2 across 14 files.

Reproducibility: not applicable. This PR is a test-fixture cleanup rather than a reported runtime bug. The observable source check is that current main still contains the old fixture names and the patch replaces them consistently with synthetic names.
commands.review_followup: - No ClawSweeper repair lane is needed because the patch has no actionable findings and should proceed through normal maintainer readiness and CI handling.

target.title: test(agents): use synthetic tool schema fixtures
target.body:
## Summary
- Replace private reported tool/plugin fixture names in schema-quarantine tests with synthetic `fuzzplugin` / `fuzz_move_angles` names.
- Keep the assertions and expected diagnostics equivalent so follow-up hardening PRs do not carry unrelated scrub noise.

## Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: test fixtures no longer use the private reported plugin/tool name while preserving the same unsupported-schema scenarios.
Real environment tested: local node-wrapper Vitest plus AWS Crabbox changed gate.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: `CI=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run extensions/codex/src/app-server/dynamic-tools.test.ts packages/gateway-protocol/src/schema/agents-models-skills.test.ts src/agents/agent-bundle-mcp-runtime.test.ts src/agents/agent-bundle-mcp-tools.materialize.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/compact.hooks.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.spawn-workspace.context-engine.test.ts src/agents/tool-schema-projection.test.ts src/agents/tools-effective-inventory.test.ts src/commands/doctor/repair-sequencing.test.ts src/commands/doctor/shared/active-tool-schema-warnings.test.ts src/commands/doctor/shared/preview-warnings.test.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.runtime.test.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.test.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.test.ts --reporter=verbose`; `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check <changed test files>`; `git diff --check`; `rg -n <forbidden reported-name pattern> <changed test files>`; `OPENCLAW_TESTBOX=1 node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --provider aws --shell -- "pnpm check:changed"`.
Evidence after fix: local node-wrapper Vitest passed 6 routed shards; oxfmt passed; diff check passed; forbidden-name and private-path scrub returned no matches; AWS Crabbox run `run_105f36331a26` on lease `cbx_105f224a59ff` passed `pnpm check:changed` with exit 0.
Observed result after fix: all changed fixtures use synthetic names and the changed test/type/lint lanes remain green.
What was not tested: no packaged or live assistant run, because this PR changes test fixture names only.

## Verification
- `CI=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run ... --reporter=verbose` over all 14 changed test files
- `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check ...`
- `git diff --check`
- `rg -n <forbidden reported-name pattern> ...` returned no matches
- AWS Crabbox `run_105f36331a26` / `cbx_105f224a59ff`: `pnpm check:changed` passed
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local` passed clean
target.author: vincentkoc
